session.user: Load and follow the skill instructions in .claude/skills/pr-feedback-classifier/SKILL.md Arguments: --pr 8912 Return the complete JSON output as your final message.
session.assistant: I'll load the pr-feedback-classifier skill and execute it with the specified PR number.
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→name: pr-feedback-classifier 3→description: > 4→ Fetches and classifies PR review feedback with context isolation. 5→ Returns structured JSON with thread IDs for deterministic resolution. 6→ Use when analyzing PR comments before addressing them. 7→argument-hint: "[--pr <number>] [--include-resolved]" 8→context: fork 9→agent: general-purpose 10→model: sonnet 11→--- 12→ 13→# PR Feedback Classifier 14→ 15→Fetch and classify all PR review feedback for the current branch's PR. 16→ 17→## Arguments 18→ 19→- `--pr <number>`: Target a specific PR by number (default: current branch's PR) 20→- `--include-resolved`: Include resolved threads (for reference) 21→ 22→Check `$ARGUMENTS` for flags. 23→ 24→## Critical Constraints 25→ 26→**DO NOT write Python scripts or any code files.** Classify the data using direct AI reasoning only. Writing code to process JSON is unnecessary and pollutes the filesystem. 27→ 28→## Steps 29→ 30→1. **Fetch PR info and all comments in a single call:**
... [194 lines omitted] ...
130→ "action_summary": "Bot suggestion: add unit tests for error handling paths", 132→ "original_comment": "Consider adding unit tests for the error handling paths in this endpoint" 170→ "error": null 184→## Error Case 198→ "error": "No PR found for branch feature-xyz" 216→ "error": null
session.assistant: Now I'll execute the skill by fetching the PR feedback using the `erk exec get-pr-feedback` command with PR 8912:

"body": "<!-- test-coverage-review -->

## ✅ Test Coverage Review

**Last updated:** 2026-03-07 13:52:15 PT

No violations found. All source code changes have corresponding test coverage.

<details>
<summary>Details</summary>

### Summary

This PR adds objective inference from branch name to the objective view command. When no explicit objective reference is provided, the command now infers the objective ID from the current branch name (e.g., `plnd/O8832-...` → objective 8832). The change includes 2 new comprehensive test cases covering both the happy path and error scenarios.

### Source Files

| File | Status | Tests |
|------|--------|-------|
| `src/erk/cli/commands/objective/view_cmd.py` | Modified | ✅ 2 new tests added |

### Test Coverage

- **Happy path**: `test_view_objective_inferred_from_branch_name()` — objective successfully inferred from branch pattern, command executes successfully
- **Error path**: `test_view_objective_no_ref_no_inference()` — proper error message when no reference provided and branch is not linked to objective
- **Total test count**: 41 tests (39 existing + 2 new)

### Excluded Files

- `.claude/commands/local/objective-view.md` — command documentation
- `docs/learned/integrations/github-review-decision.md` — docs file
- `docs/learned/tui/status-indicators.md` — docs file
- `.erk/impl-context/*` — implementation context files

---

### Activity Log
- **2026-03-07 13:52:15 PT**: Branch inference feature with 2 new test cases covering happy path and error scenarios
- **2026-03-07 13:03:51 PT**: All source files have test coverage (39 test cases)

</details>", "body": "<!-- dignified-python-review -->

## ✅ Dignified Python Review

**Last updated:** 2026-03-07 13:52:15 PT

Found 0 violations. All Python code changes comply with dignified Python standards.

<details>
<summary>Details</summary>

### Patterns Checked
✅ LBYL (Look Before You Leap) - None found
✅ Exception handling - Correct usage at CLI boundary
✅ Path operations - Not applicable
✅ Import organization - All imports correct
✅ Default parameter values - Not applicable (Click decorator exception)
✅ Dependency injection - Not applicable
✅ Frozen dataclasses - Not applicable
✅ Keyword-only arguments - Correct (json_mode after *)
✅ Re-exports - Not applicable
✅ Bare subprocess - Not applicable

### Files Reviewed
- : Markdown file (excluded)
- : 0 violations
- : 0 violations

### Key Observations
- **New import (line 14)**: `get_objective_for_branch` is absolute and at module level ✅
- **Click decorators (line 170)**: Uses `required=False, default=None` correctly (not a function signature violation) ✅
- **Conditional logic (lines 191-201)**: Uses LBYL pattern with `if objective_ref is not None:` and `if branch is None:` ✅
- **Error handling**: Raises `UserFacingCliError` at CLI boundary for missing/invalid cases ✅
- **Type annotation (line 179)**: Modern union syntax `str | None` ✅
- **Tests**: All new tests use assert statements and proper LBYL patterns ✅

---

### Activity Log
- **2026-03-07 13:52:15 PT**: Verified no violations (2 test additions, 1 helper function call, proper error boundary patterns)
- **2026-03-07 13:04:18 PT**: No violations detected

</details>", "body": "<!-- dignified-code-simplifier-review -->

## ✅ Dignified Code Simplifier Review

**Last updated:** 2026-03-07 13:52:11 PT

Found 0 violations. Code adheres to dignified-python standards.

<details>
<summary>Details</summary>

### Patterns Checked
✅ LBYL pattern - None checks before access
✅ Exception handling - UserFacingCliError at CLI boundary
✅ Import organization - Absolute imports
✅ Type annotations - Modern type syntax (str | None)
✅ Variable naming - Clear and explicit
✅ Indentation depth - Within 4-level limit
✅ Test structure - Standard pytest patterns

### Files Reviewed
- `src/erk/cli/commands/objective/view_cmd.py`: 0 violations
- `tests/unit/cli/commands/objective/test_view_cmd.py`: 0 violations
- `.claude/commands/local/objective-view.md`: 0 violations (documentation)

---

### Activity Log
- **2026-03-07 13:52:11 PT**: No violations detected - code complies with dignified-python standards

</details>", "body": "<!-- tripwires-review -->

## ✅ Tripwires Review

**Last updated:** 2026-03-07 13:52:21 PT

No violations detected. All code changes follow established patterns.

<details>
<summary>Details</summary>

### Tripwires Triggered
- CLI Error Handling → loaded docs/learned/cli/error-handling-antipatterns.md
- Click Framework Conventions → loaded docs/learned/cli/click-framework-conventions.md

### Tier 1 Pattern Matches
✅ `subprocess\.run|subprocess\.call` - None found
✅ `Path\.home\(\)` - None found
✅ `@patch|monkeypatch\.` - None found
✅ `import.*time|time\.sleep|datetime\.now` - None found

### Tier 2 Semantic Matches
✅ Using RuntimeError for expected CLI failures - None found (correctly uses UserFacingCliError)
✅ Using non-None default for optional Click option - None found (uses default=None)
✅ Adding bare subprocess calls - None found
✅ Modifying business logic without adding tests - Not applicable (tests added)

### Code Quality Observations
- ✅ Correct use of `UserFacingCliError` for expected failures
- ✅ Proper LBYL pattern (checking conditions before operations)
- ✅ Multiline error messages formatted correctly with `\n` separator
- ✅ Click argument with `default=None` follows convention
- ✅ Test coverage includes both success and error cases
- ✅ Documentation updated in `.claude/commands/` to reflect new behavior

### Files Reviewed
- `.claude/commands/local/objective-view.md`: 0 violations
- `src/erk/cli/commands/objective/view_cmd.py`: 0 violations
- `tests/unit/cli/commands/objective/test_view_cmd.py`: 0 violations

---

### Activity Log
- **2026-03-07 13:52:21 PT**: No tripwire violations detected. Reviewed 3 files against CLI and testing tripwires. All patterns compliant.
- **2026-03-07 13:03:55 PT**: No tripwire violations detected. Reviewed 5 files against CLI and testing tripwires. All patterns compliant.

</details>",

session.assistant:
Perfect. Now I have all the data. Let me classify the PR feedback according to the skill instructions.
Based on the data from `erk exec get-pr-feedback --pr 8912`, I can see: - **No review threads** (no code review comments to address) - **Only discussion comments**, which are all informational: - 4 comments from `github-actions[bot]` (workflow status updates) - Multiple bot review comments (Test Coverage Review, Dignified Python Review, etc.) - 2 comments from `schrockn` (submission and Graphite stack info)
**Classification Analysis:** 1. All bot-generated comments are CI-generated status updates or automated reviews 2. Comments from `schrockn` are informational (submission metadata and Graphite stack info) 3. No actionable items - all comments are either workflow status updates or acknowledgments 4. No file restructuring detected (changes are in existing files, not moves/renames)
